entry.title: RAP Fiori Application Works in ADT Preview but Not Correctly in Fiori Launchpad (SAP Public cloud)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have developed an RAP Excel Upload application using RAP in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using ABAP Development Tools (ADT Eclipse) only, without using SAP Business Application Studio.

WHAT IS WORKING

In ADT Eclipse, when I click Preview on the Service Binding, the application works perfectly.

All features are working correctly:

  • Create upload record
  • Upload Excel file
  • Download template
  • Process Excel
  • Header data is visible
  • Item data is visible
  • Navigation between Header and Item levels works correctly

    WHAT I DID

    1. Created the Fiori application using Quick Fiori Application Generator in ADT.
    2. Transported the objects to the Test system.
    3. Created:
      • IAM App
      • Business Catalog
    4. Assigned the catalog to the required business role.

    Now the application appears in the Fiori Launchpad.


    ISSUE

    Although the application opens in the Fiori Launchpad, it does not behave the same way as in ADT Preview.

    In ADT Preview, I can see the navigation arrow (>) to expand and navigate between Header and Item tables.

    However, in the Fiori Launchpad version, the navigation arrow is not visible, so I cannot navigate from Header to Item records.

    Because of this, the application is not functioning as expected.

    Screenshot

    Below is the screen where the Fiori app was generated in ADT.

    This screen shot is from the ADT preview here it is working correctly



    This one is from the Fiori Launchpad which is not working correctly

     

     



    Question

    Could someone please help me understand:

    • Why the navigation arrow is visible in ADT Preview but not in Fiori Launchpad?
    • Is there any additional configuration required for RAP associations or Fiori elements navigation in S/4HANA Public Cloud?

    Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

    Thank you!

entry.title: Re: What contains under unused_data of disk_overview query
entry.content:
Hi Ameya,

"UNUSED_GB" is basically the difference between TOTAL_GB and the USED_GB size of the allocated space assigned by the hana/data volume.
FRAG_PCT represents this difference.

As explained in the header of the 'HANA_Disks_Overview.txt' script in Note https://me.sap.com/notes/1969700

- TOTAL_GB: Total space (GB)
- USED_GB: Used space (GB)
- UNUSED_GB: Unused space (GB)
- FRAG_PCT: Fragmentation (%), unused space vs. total space


Kind Regards
Simon

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 - Edição dos 10 anos do SITREC
entry.content:
Inscrições em breve

 

Celebrando 10 anos do SAP Inside Track Recife, desde sua primeira edição em 2016, reforçamos a força de uma comunidade que cresce com colaboração, troca de conhecimento e conexões genuínas no Nordeste. Inserido em uma jornada nacional iniciada em 2009, o evento percorreu diversas cidades, como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Curitiba, Porto Alegre, Belo Horizonte, Fortaleza, Salvador, Campinas, Ribeirão Preto e São José dos Campos, consolidando o SAP Inside Track como um movimento que vai além da tecnologia, é sobre pessoas, experiências e a construção contínua de um ecossistema SAP cada vez mais forte no Brasil.

Veja como foi a ultima edição ano passado: Blog SITREC2025

Acompanhe palestras anteriores aqui neste link: SAP Inside Track Recife - Playlist


A agenda do evento 2026 ainda não foi divulgada.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track Recife é a maior oportunidade de aprender, compartilhar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Teremos palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA.

Além disso, este é o momento perfeito para expandir seu networking e conhecer pessoalmente grandes profissionais da Comunidade SAP e das redes sociais.

Veja como foram as edições passadas neste link -> #SITREC

Data: 23/05/2026

Local: Hotel Manibu Recife - Avenida Conselheiro Aguiar - Boa Viagem, Recife - PE, Brasil

Inscrição: Não liberada (Inscrições para aquisição de ingressos no Eventbrite)

Chamada para Palestrantes

Se você tem conhecimento para compartilhar, esta é sua oportunidade! Envie sua proposta com o tema, título e uma sinopse para: sapinsidetrackbrazil@gmail.com  o tempo de palestra é de no máximo 40 minutos, e o último dia para envio de propostas é em 05 de Agosto de 2024. Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe com a confirmação.

Agenda: A definir

Sobre Recife

Recife, a capital do estado de Pernambuco, carrega em seu nome e história a essência das características geográficas e culturais que marcam a região. A palavra "Recife" deriva do termo árabe "rasif", que significa "cais" ou "quebra-mar", uma referência direta à formação natural dos arrecifes de coral que protegem sua costa e criam as famosas piscinas naturais. Por sua vez, o nome "Pernambuco" tem origem no tupi, com as palavras "paranã-puka" ou "para-nã-mbuk", traduzidas como "mar furado" ou "buraco no mar", também referenciando as passagens naturais existentes nos arrecifes. Tanto Recife quanto Pernambuco refletem, em suas etimologias, uma conexão intrínseca com o mar e com as formações naturais que moldaram não apenas sua geografia, mas também sua identidade cultural.

Hoje, Recife é uma das cidades mais antigas do Brasil e se destaca como a quarta mais desenvolvida entre os mercados emergentes globais. A capital abriga o Porto Digital, o maior parque tecnológico do Brasil, e possui o segundo maior polo médico do país, consolidando-se como um centro de inovação e excelência em saúde. Seu aeroporto internacional, reconhecido por sua qualidade, conecta a cidade ao mundo, enquanto sua rica cultura gastronômica, fortemente influenciada por séculos de miscigenação, atrai visitantes de todas as partes.

Combinando uma infraestrutura de ponta com uma vibrante cena cultural e tecnológica, Recife é muito mais do que uma cidade: é um símbolo de resiliência e inovação. A cidade, assim como o estado de Pernambuco, que a abriga, mantém vivas as marcas de sua história enquanto projeta um futuro de oportunidades, consolidando-se como um destino privilegiado para negócios, turismo e eventos de relevância nacional e internacional.

Organizadores e Patrocínio

O SAP Inside Track Recife é organizado por membros dedicados da Comunidade SAP, com apoio de diversos patrocinadores que tornam este evento possível. Gostaríamos de agradecer a todos que contribuem para o sucesso deste encontro.

Apoio: A definir

Junte-se a nós no SAP Inside Track Recife 2026!

Esperamos vê-lo lá para um dia repleto de aprendizado, networking e inovação.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus - Pune (March 2026) Recap
entry.content:
What started as a plan to bring the community back together turned into a morning full of ideas, questions, and some very honest conversations around AI in the SAP space. Safe to say, the energy in the room did not disappoint! 🙌

A quick look at what we covered:

• From AI Experiments to an Enterprise AI Operating System
A strong reminder that AI success is not about more models, but better data and clearer foundations. Moving from experiments to real enterprise adoption needs structure.

• AI-Powered Extensions on SAP BTP
A very real example of cleaning and optimizing large-scale master data using a multi-agent approach. If there was one takeaway here, it’s this: bad data still causes most problems.

• ITOps Copilot
AI stepping into IT operations with LLM-driven ticket analysis. Faster insights, less manual effort, and a glimpse into how support teams might work going forward.

• SAP Document AI in Action
Invoices going from paper to structured SAP entries with minimal human intervention. Simple idea, big impact.

• The Future of Intelligent ERP
AI, Joule, and the “right data, right process, right time” approach shaping what the next generation of ERP could look like.

But beyond the sessions, what really stood out was the community itself. Conversations that continued between sessions, people exchanging notes, and a room that stayed engaged throughout.

Also, putting this together was just as much fun as attending it. Always a good sign! 💪

A big thank you to our speakers, participants, and everyone who helped make this happen behind the scenes.

We’re just getting started with Stammtisch Plus in Pune. More to come! Stay tuned!

Scroll for some glimpses of the event!

Organizers & Volunteers:

  • @Parineeta_Somvanshi 
  • @Punith_Oswal 
  • @Asutosh21 

#StammPune

Best Regards,
SAP Community Group – Pune 


Our Speakers!Our Volunteers!

entry.title: ZBV / CUA is not working as expected
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm currently having a strange issue with the CUA. Everything is working perfectly in our CUA system. We don't have duplicate company addresses, and users are assigned the correct one. Our Q-system is also working as expected. We are currently on S4/HANA 2023 SP 03/2025.

But here’s the problem: We’re currently having issues with our P-system. In this system, there are several users assigned to a different company; for example, the correct company address would be 41000 – Standard Company, and now we have about 90 addressesSAP with a new number and a new description -> 41001 – Standard Company0000012345, etc.

We have deleted or corrected these incorrect company addresses in the CUA system, the Dev system, and the Q system, but we cannot delete them in the P system. Do you have any idea how we can delete these incorrect entries? I tried using transaction SUCOMP, but it doesn’t work. I receive the following error message when I try to delete a company address, and the address we want to use is the default address:

And even if I assign a new company to the user, I still can't delete that incorrect company.
Sincerely, Dominik 🙂

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：高级外币评估中的跨币种清账处理
entry.content:
业务背景

本篇博客旨在说明和探讨SAP近年推出的高级外币评估功能如何处理实际业务中可能发生的跨币种清账业务。

存在跨币种清账数据的场景下，由于交易货币 (00) 可能不同于公司代码货币 (10) 和集团货币 (30)，在发票凭证被收付款清算后，相关已清项可能还会列示在高级外币评估中，生成外币评估分录，以体现汇率变动对外币业务数据的影响。

 

系统测试

测试用例前提条件：公司代码货币CNY；集团货币USD，且设置集团货币由公司代码货币换算得出。

测试系统的货币汇率设置如下：

 

示例1：

交易货币层面：应付账款 10,000 USD，付款清账 69,755.00 CNY，跨月清账

 

发票凭证：

应付账款 10,000 USD，过账日期 2025-12-28，USD/CNY = 6.33745

 

付款凭证：

付款清账69,755.00 CNY，过账日期 2026-01-20，USD/CNY = 6.97550

 

期间1执行高级外币评估：

分析关账运行结果（F3753），运行日期：2025-12-31，USD/CNY = 6.97550

测试运行

正式运行

 

查看期间1生成的日记账分录：

 

期间2执行高级外币评估：

分析关账运行结果（F3753），运行日期：2026-01-31，USD/CNY = 6.90410

测试运行

正式运行

应付账款10,000 USD，基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.33745 得出公司代码货币金额 = 63,375.40 CNY；同时得出集团货币金额 = 10,000 USD。

付款清账 69,755.00 CNY，基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.97550 得出集团货币金额 = 10,000 USD。

上述两笔分录在公司代码金额层面相差 6,380 CNY，由于在公司代码货币层面存在汇兑损益差额，所以列示在高级外币评估中 (如上图)。

 

查看期间2生成的日记账分录：

按照不同货币类型查看日记账分录：

 

业务凭证流梳理如下：

 

示例2：

交易货币：应付账款 10,000 EUR，付款清账 12,379.44 USD，当月清账

 

发票凭证：

应付账款 10,000 EUR，过账日期2025-12-28，EUR/CNY = 7.84541，USD/CNY = 6.33745

 

付款凭证：

付款清账 12,379.44 USD，过账日期 2025-12-29，USD/CNY = 6.33745

 

执行高级外币评估：

分析关账运行结果（F3753），运行日期：2025-12-31，EUR/CNY = 8.34360，USD/CNY = 6.97550

测试运行

正式运行

应付账款10,000 EUR，基于货币汇率 EUR/CNY = 7.84541 得出公司代码货币金额 = 78,454.10 CNY；基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.33745得出集团货币金额 = 12,379.44 USD。

付款清账 12,379.44 USD，基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.33745 得出公司代码货币金额 = 78,454.08 CNY；同时得出集团货币金额 = 12,379.44 USD。

上述两笔分录在公司代码金额层面相差 0.02 CNY，由于在公司代码货币层面存在汇兑损益差额，所以列示在高级外币评估中 (如上图)。

 

查看高级外币评估生成的日记账分录：

 

业务凭证流梳理如下：

 

总结

从上述示例中可以看出，汇率差异体现在公司代码货币 (10) 或者集团货币 (30) 下，会通过高级外币评估功能生成日记账分录，借贷源科目（原始统驭科目）以体现汇率波动的影响，但调整的原始统驭科目是S类型总账科目，不会影响客户或者供应商实际未清项的清算数据。

综上所述，SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition系统中的高级外币评估功能能够处理跨币种清账业务，在每个期间，按照期末汇率过账汇兑损益重估分录，以体现汇率波动对于此类业务的影响。

entry.title: Extend Your S-User Expiry Date (and Avoid Last-Minute Surprises)
entry.content:
If you’ve been receiving email notifications about your S-user ID approaching its expiry date, there are reminders as part of SAP’s S-user lifetime management, designed to ensure that only active and required users continue to access SAP systems. 

In this blog, we’ll walk you through what an S-user ID is, why expiry matters, and most importantly, how to extend the expiry date so your access remains uninterrupted. 

 

What Is an S-user ID? 

An S-user ID is a unique identifier that allows you to log in to https://me.sap.com and perform key activities such as: 

  • Reporting support cases 
  • Downloading software 
  • Generating license keys 
  • Configuring system connections 
  • And much more 

An S-user ID always starts with the letter “S” followed by numbers (for example: S0xxxxxxxxx). 

 

Why Does the S-user Expiry Date Matter? 

S-user IDs come with an expiry date, which is managed exclusively by your company’s all Administrators, they can edit the lifetime of an S-user ID. 

If the expiry date is not extended in time, the following happens: 

  1. The S-user ID is first deactivated (status set to Expired). 
  1. In a second step, the S-user ID may be deleted entirely. 

That’s why proactive management of S-user lifetimes is so important. 

Who Can Manage the Expiry Date? 

Only the following roles can manage S-user expiry dates in the User Management application: 

  • Super Administrators 
  • Cloud Administrators 
  • User Administrators 

A few important rules to keep in mind: 

  • Regular S-users: 
  • SAP recommends extending access for up to 24 months. 
  • In special cases, expiry can be extended for up to 60 months. 
  • Administrators: 
  • All administrator accounts are set to never expire 
  • The Manage button is intentionally inactive for administrators (see KBA note# 3205123 - Manage or Change Expiry Date is greyed out on an administrator S-user ID - SAP for Me). 
  • Validity limits: 
  • Shortest possible validity: 1 day 
  • Longest possible validity: 60 months 
  • When updating multiple S-users at once, again range is 1 to 24 months (see KBA 2931034- How to mass extend the Expiration Date for a group of S-user IDs - SAP for Me). 

 

 

How to Extend the S-user Expiry Date 

Once logged in to User Management, administrators have several ways to extend an S-user’s expiry date. 

 

Option 1: Use the “Manage Expiry” 
 

  • Locate the S-user in the Users list. 
  • Click the Manage Expiry icon. 
  • Select the new expiry date and save. 
     
     

     

You’ll see a confirmation message: 

“The expiry date for the S-user has been changed successfully.” 

The user status will briefly show “Change Requested”, and after a few minutes, it will return to Active with the updated expiry date visible. 

 

Option 2: Use the “Change Expiry Date” Button 

  • Select the checkbox next to the S-user. 
  • Click Change Expiry Date above the Users list. 
  • Choose the new expiry date and click Save. 

     

     

     

This process follows the same confirmation and status update as Option 1. 

 

Option 3: Extend from the S-user Details Page 

  • Click on the S-user ID to open the details page. 
  • Select Change Expiry Date in the top-right corner. 
  • Choose the new expiry date and click Save. 

 

 

Again, the update will process within a few minutes. 

Managing your S-user expiry proactively ensures smooth access, uninterrupted productivity, and a better overall SAP experience.  

 

Need Help? We’re Here for You 

The  Customer Interaction Center  is always happy to guide you through these steps and answer any questions you may have. Don’t hesitate to reach out if you need assistance. 

 

 Thanks for reading!  
 
We hope you found this helpful 
 
We would love to hear from you, let us know what you think? 

 
Read more Customer Interaction Center blog posts Here 

 If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

entry.title: Re: How to Create an Object List Page in SAP Fiori Elements – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
@deepak_h3  Ji, 
Thank You for your Comment.

Yes, you can use @OData.publish: true to expose the CDS view as an OData service. Once the CDS is activated, SAP automatically generates the corresponding OData service, which can then be registered in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.

In most real-time projects, @OData.publish: true is generally not used. It is mainly helpful for demo, learning, and quick testing purpose only.

entry.title: Re: RAP Fiori Application Works in ADT Preview but Not Co...
entry.content: This usually happens because ADT Preview runs with generated metadata, while the Fiori Launchpad depends on fully exposed annotations and service configuration. Check that your RAP BO has proper associations with @ui.lineItem and @ui.facet annotations enabling navigation. Ensure the child entity is exposed in the projection view and service definition. Also verify @Consumption.semanticObject and navigation settings are maintained. In Public Cloud, missing draft or composition settings can hide navigation arrows. Finally, clear FLP cache and refresh metadata. If the issue persists, regenerate the Fiori app and confirm the service binding is correctly activated and published.

entry.title: Live Session: Die SAP BTP entdecken – deutschsprachiger Beginner‑Guide
entry.content:
Live Session: Die SAP BTP entdecken – deutschsprachiger Beginner‑Guide

Die SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) gewinnt in immer mehr Projekten an Bedeutung. Gleichzeitig stellt sich für viele Einsteiger die Frage, wie man einen einfachen Zugang zur Plattform findet – gerade, wenn man sich erstmals mit Cloud‑Services, Subaccounts oder modernen SAP‑Architekturen beschäftigt.

Um diesen Einstieg möglichst verständlich zu gestalten, wird eine deutschsprachige Live‑Session für komplette Beginner angeboten:

🇩🇪 „A guide to SAP BTP for complete beginners - language DE“ 🇩🇪

📅  Freitag, 15. Mai 2026, 10 Uhr
🕒  Live & kostenlos
Anmeldung: https://url.sap/7p6bmk

 

Was bietet die Session?

Die Session vermittelt einen allgemeinen Überblick über die SAP BTP – in klarer, verständlicher und einsteigerfreundlicher Form.
Sie richtet sich an alle, die:

  • neu in der SAP BTP sind
  • eine Einführung auf Deutsch bevorzugen
  • neugierig sind, welche Möglichkeiten die Plattform bietet
  • einen unkomplizierten Einstieg suchen, ohne technische Tiefe

Der Fokus liegt darauf, die Plattform zugänglicher zu machen und eine Orientierung zu geben, die den weiteren Lernweg erleichtert.

 

Für wen ist die Session geeignet?

Für Beginner jeder Art:
Consultants, Key User oder alle, die sich erstmals mit der SAP BTP beschäftigen und einen leichten, geführten Einstieg suchen.

Einladung an die Community

Interessierte sind herzlich eingeladen, an der Session teilzunehmen, Fragen zu stellen und die Gelegenheit zu nutzen, einen ersten Eindruck von der SAP BTP zu gewinnen -  ohne Vorwissen und ohne Stress.

👉 Zur Anmeldung: https://url.sap/7p6bmk

entry.title: State of the SAP BusinessObjects BI Upgrade - March 2026
entry.content:
Lovely people, it's been a while since we took a look at the State of the SAP BusinessObjects BI Upgrade in July 2025. The big news since then was the release of SAP's February 2026 Security Note, which revealed four CVE in both SAP BI 4.3 and BI 2025. The good news was the note indicated that BI 4.3 SP5 patch 4 and BI 2025 SP0 Patch 8 would eliminate the vulnerabilities. What was unknown at the time is that both releases were affected by a clustering issue described in SAP Note 3724948, Publications fail / cluster communication fails after upgrade to 4.3 SP05 Patch 400 / BI 2025 Patch 800.

SAP BI 2025 SP0 Patch 9 was released on March 17, 2026, and SAP BI 4.3 SP5 Patch 5 will be available "any day now", as its release has been delayed so it can include the clustering fix. SAP BI 2025 SP0 Patch 9 also restores Corporate Color Palette functionality for Web Intelligence and includes many other fixes across the platform.

While on the topic of security, there are still organizations trying to manage with SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.2, which has been out of patch support since 2022. Over the course of its lifetime, BI 4.2 was flagged with 38 common vulnerabilities and exposures (CVE). Regardless of which patch level of BI 4.2 your organization is using, it’s likely to be affected by at least one of these and probably more. If you're in this predicament, just a reminder that there is no ROI on a security breach. Be sure to review both the risks of remaining on BI 4.2 as well as the benefits to moving to BI 4.3/BI 2025 with your information security team.

Stay safe. Stay secure. And stay up-to-date, giving your users the productivity improvements available in the latest versions of the SAP BusinessObjects platform.

entry.title: SAP PAPM and SAP Business Data Cloud - A Data-Centric Approach to Direct Tax
entry.content:
Picture 1. Jurisdiction tax analysis

MOTIVATION

In today's fast-paced business world, the ability to effectively steer and lead an organization is fundamental for achieving strategic goals and maintaining a competitive edge. A key success criterion is the capacity to fully harness and derive value from organizational data. Many companies struggle with data problems, such as fragmented information, outdated systems, and the super important task of preparing data for emerging technologies like AI. Recognizing these challenges, we are excited to introduce a blog series dedicated to showcasing the substantial business value of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) in the context of planning and analytics.

The motivation behind this initiative is to provide a comprehensive exploration of how SAP BDC capabilities as essential instruments, facilitating robust strategic alignment and guiding effective decision-making processes across diverse organizational requirements. We aim to illustrate how SAP BDC, equipped with powerful data management, planning, analytics and innovative AI capabilities, empowers organizations with enhanced business steering agility by providing real-time insights, streamlined data management, and automated decision support – going from insights to action, helps mainly leaders guide their businesses with precision.

Our blog series will provide valuable insights for decision-makers seeking to harness SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) to enhance operational efficiency, optimize resource allocation, and achieve long-term objectives. By featuring detailed planning & analytics topics, the series will illustrate the transformative impact of SAP BDC in addressing the operational complexities of today’s business environment. This first introductory blog post will spotlight the strategic advantages of SAP BDC, particularly in the context of Business Steering, setting the stage for a deeper exploration of its benefits throughout the blog series.

There are several blog posts in the series that will cover advantages of SAP BDC in the context of business steering, so please stay tuned for the upcoming blogs:

  1. The Value of SAP BDC in the context of business steering 
  2.  Planning & Analytics is an essential part of SAP BDC  
  3. Key Planning & Analytics components of SAP BDC 
  4. Is AI going to “retire” a dashboard?
  5. Simulations, a game changer in today’s volatile world
  6. Extend existing planning scenarios of SAP BPC/BW-IP in SAP BDC 
  7. Real-time steering with LIVE planning
  8. SAP PaPM - The role of business calculation engine in business steering 
  9. Self – service analytics and real time business steering
  10. Business steering powered by AI agents
  11. Leverage data products in the context of planning & forecasting
  12. Introducing AI/ML capabilities by SAP Databricks
  13. SAP PAPM and SAP Business Data Cloud - A Data-Centric Approach to Direct Tax (THIS BLOG POST)

INTRODUCTION

Why are we discussing tax in this series, and how does it relate to SAP Business Data Cloud?

Direct tax functions are facing increasing pressure driven by regulatory complexity, shorter reporting cycles, and rising expectations around transparency and auditability. These challenges are not new, but their cumulative impact is becoming harder to manage with fragmented processes and data structures.

Process automation is often seen as a way to address these issues. However, in the context of direct tax, automation is rarely effective on its own. Many limitations stem not from the absence of tools, but from underlying data inconsistencies, limited governance, and restricted reuse of tax-relevant data across systems and processes. Without a solid data foundation, automation efforts tend to remain isolated and require ongoing manual intervention.

From this perspective, SAP Business Data Cloud provides a framework for addressing direct tax challenges by emphasizing data consistency, integration, and reuse. Rather than treating tax as a downstream reporting activity, it enables tax-relevant data to be managed alongside financial and operational data, supporting more reliable calculations, reporting, and compliance processes.

This approach delivers several tangible benefits:

  • Improved data accuracy and consistency across tax, finance, and operational datasets

  • Enhanced auditability and transparency, with clear data lineage and traceability

  • Greater reuse of tax-relevant data, avoiding duplication across systems and processes

  • Stronger alignment between tax and finance, enabling more consistent reporting outcomes

This is why, within SAP, direct tax is increasingly discussed in connection with EPM applications and a shared data foundation. Combining process logic with a consistent data layer helps align financial, tax, and sustainability information from an end-to-end process perspective. It allows organizations to build on ERP data they already rely on, while extending it in a controlled way to support the specific requirements of global tax.

WHERE DATA MEETS A PREDEFINED TAX APPLICATION

When looking at direct tax processes, it quickly becomes clear that many elements are highly standardized. Regulatory frameworks define calculation logic, data requirements, and reporting structures with little room for interpretation. This makes direct tax a strong candidate for predefined, application-based approaches rather than purely bespoke implementations.

This is the approach taken with an SAP PaPM–powered application for direct tax management. The application covers the main steps of the direct tax lifecycle—from data collection and income tax determination to BEPS Pillar Two calculations, reporting, and audit support—while remaining flexible enough to adapt to organization-specific requirements.

Moreover, SAP PaPM enables a creation of a pro-active transfer pricing management framework. While BEPS Pillar Two focuses on minimum taxation and jurisdiction-level Top-Up Tax calculations, Operational Transfer Pricing (OTP) addresses how profits are allocated through intercompany pricing and year-end adjustments. Both areas rely on the underlying financial and transactional data and directly influence each other’s outcomes—as well as the overall bottom line.

This interdependency makes a shared data foundation, provided by SAP Business Data Cloud, particularly important. SAP PaPM runs directly in SAP Datasphere or SAP Hana Cloud and complements this foundation by offering a common calculation and process framework that allows OTP and Pillar Two to be aligned within a single, consistent logic, improving transparency, consistency, and governance across tax processes.

Picture 2. Architecture

To illustrate this, the following sections walk through a BEPS Pillar Two calculation process as it can look like in an SAP-based environment.

Structuring the Pillar Two Process

The process starts from a role-based launchpad, in this example designed for a role of global tax manager. Rather than exposing all technical details, the launchpad focuses on the key steps required in day-to-day user work: data collection, manual adjustments, Safe Harbour tests, elections, and the Top-Up Tax calculation itself.

As a first step master data and annual configuration data play a central role here. These parameters drive the BEPS Pillar Two calculations and can be reviewed and adjusted centrally, ensuring transparency and consistency across jurisdictions and reporting periods.

Picture 3. BEPS Pillar 2 launchpad

Managing Financial Data and Adjustments

Once the configuration is in place, the process moves into financial data processing. BEPS Pillar Two requires significant volumes of data from multiple sources, and many tax-relevant figures must be reviewed or adjusted to align with GloBE definitions.

Manual adjustments are therefore an unavoidable part of the process. The key challenge is not eliminating them, but managing them in a structured and auditable way. In this setup, adjustments are made within a governed environment, with predefined validations, mandatory comments, and a full change history.

For example, tax items can be reviewed by company and scenario. If an adjustment is required—such as modifying related-party revenue for a specific entity—the user can drill down to the relevant tax item, enter the adjustment, and document the rationale. Built-in validations immediately flag inconsistencies, such as incorrect currencies or unusual values, allowing experts to correct or consciously approve deviations.

All changes are traceable: who made them, when, and why. This significantly reduces reliance on external spreadsheets and email-based explanations.

Picture 4. Manual adjustments

Process Transparency and Collaboration

Beyond individual data entries, BEPS Pillar Two is a collaborative, multi-step process. Different roles are involved in data preparation, review, approval, and final calculation.

A process management view provides transparency across these steps. It shows what tasks are pending, who is responsible, and whether approvals are required. This helps avoid the typical coordination challenges that arise when BEPS Pillar Two is managed across disconnected tools and documents.

Picture 5. Process management

From Safe Harbour Checks to Final Results

Once data inputs, adjustments, and elections are completed, the solution supports Safe Harbour assessments. Results can be analyzed at both company and jurisdiction level, showing which jurisdictions pass the Safe Harbour tests and which do not.

For jurisdictions that fail, the system provides an estimated Top-Up Tax result. While this is not the final calculation, it offers early insight into potential exposure and helps tax teams prioritize their efforts.

The full OECD-aligned Top-Up Tax calculation is then performed, considering all configurations, data inputs, and elections. Results are available at jurisdiction and company level, including the allocation of Top-Up Tax under QDMTT and IIR rules and the mechanism through which the tax is payable.

Additional analytical dimensions—such as region or business unit—can be applied to support internal reporting needs.

Picture 6. Top-up Tax allocation results

An important aspect of BEPS Pillar Two compliance is the ability to maintain multiple calculation versions. The solution supports parallel runs, comparisons, and a complete audit trail. This is essential not only for audits, but also for recalculations, restatements, and future regulatory changes.

The focus is not limited to the current reporting cycle, but on creating a sustainable setup that can evolve over time.

In this example, we can see mostly BEPS Pillar Two related data, but as explained in the introduction, SAP PAPM allows you to create a tax management framework, connecting Operational Transfer Pricing and BEPS Pillar Two and supporting forward-looking analysis. As an example, tax teams can simulate how changes in transfer pricing policies or margins affect effective tax rates and potential Top-Up Tax exposure. This moves BEPS Pillar Two from a purely compliance-driven exercise toward a more controlled and proactive tax management process and generates even more value for business users.

Insights and AI-Supported Analysis

While detailed results can be reviewed step by step, summarized dashboards provide a consolidated view of key metrics, such as Safe Harbour status or CbCR-related indicators.

At this point, AI-supported interaction becomes relevant. With Joule embedded into the process, users can ask natural-language questions—such as requesting a summary of BEPS Pillar Two results or identifying jurisdictions with the highest Top-Up Tax. Because these insights are derived from governed, tax-specific data, they support data-driven decision-making without replacing expert judgment.

Picture 7. Joule and AI powered analysis

This highlights the role of SAP Business Data Cloud as an enabler for analytics and AI: insights are only as reliable as the underlying data foundation.

There are also many developments to look forward to on the AI roadmap. Planned features include capabilities such as LLM functions with grounding, enabling the derivation of applicable tax rules based on accounting document descriptions and providing relevant recommendations. Additional innovations include automated process and model generation, as well as a knowledge graph function that will, for example, help validate derived tax rules against the organization’s compliance framework. These advancements are expected to significantly expand the possibilities for AI-driven analysis and automation.

CONCLUSION: PROCESS AND DATA AS EQUAL ENABLERS

The example shown demonstrates an end-to-end BEPS Pillar Two solution that supports tax data management, Safe Harbour assessments, elections, and jurisdiction-level Top-Up Tax calculations within a single, structured framework.

Challenges such as fragmented data, manual reconciliations, and limited transparency are addressed through a combination of predefined process logic and a shared data foundation. Rather than focusing on automation alone, the approach emphasizes consistency, traceability, and reuse of trusted ERP data. It is also important to highlight that SAP PaPM is an agnostic solution. Data can be collected and integrated from multiple sources, including both SAP and non-SAP systems.

This is where SAP PaPM, application-based tax content, and SAP Business Data Cloud complement each other: process logic and data foundation work together to support compliance, analysis, and future adaptability in an increasingly complex tax and EPM landscape.

USEFUL LINKS

In case you are interested in knowing more please refer to our help content for SAP PAPM.

Moreover I would recommend checking other SAP powered tax and analytical solutions to get a full perspective about SAP capabilities in these areas.
