entry.title: Creating Electronic Documents In Contract Accounting and Convergent Invoicing
entry.content:
This blog post is for developers and consultants, introducing the procedure how to create electronic documents (eDocuments) in Contract Accounting and Convergent Invoicing that are extended from the Financial Accounting and Sales and Distribution modules in SAP S/4HANA. Since it is an extension from the FI/SD modules, the customizing and processes of eDocuments for specific countries should already exist in the system.

Prerequisites

Customizing set up by Financial Accounting
(No action required for the users using Contract Accounting)

  1. Define the process steps in the eDocument Process (transaction: EDOP).
  2. Make sure that the FI/SD localization is available for creating electronic documents in Contract Accounting and Invoicing.

SAP Note Implementation for Contract Accounting or Convergent Invoicing

  1. For creating contract accounting electronic documents, implement the following SAP Notes:
    • 3342537
    • 3331737 (For non-Peppol countries)
    • 3381485 (For Peppol countries)
  2. For creating electronic documents in Convergent invoicing, activate event 2648 in the transaction FQEVENTS and assign FM FKC_EVENT_2648.
  3. For both Contract Accounting and Invoicing, you have set up the value mapping based on your business requirements.
    • First create your value mapping in the transaction /n/AIF/CUST.
    • Then fill with values in the value mapping in the transaction /N/AIF/VMAP.

How to Extend eDocument Creation Function from the FI/SD module?

Change the Method of Class

In the class builder transaction SE24, enter the class based on your application component as the Object Type, and change the implementation of CREATE_EDOC_INSTANCES method.
Note: For Contract Accounting, use the CL_EDOC_FACTORY_FICA_DOC class; for Convergent Invoicing, use the CL_EDOC_FACTORY_CI_DOCUM class.

Create your logic for eDocument create, you can find the example as follows:

  • You can create your own logic by creating a custom method with the prefix "Z" on line 12 and line 16 as follows. By doing so, you have your own customized method based on the SAP delivered method.

    You can find how to create a new class in the following setting.

  • Alternatively, you can also develop your own factory class. Meanwhile you must make sure that you add the factory class is maintained in the view EDOFACTORYV in the transaction SM30.

Determine eDocument Type for Contract Accounting Documents

Maintain the TFK003EDOC2 table. Determine eDocument type for contract accounting document with the accounting document type as shown in the following screen:

Assign eDocument Type to Convergent Invoice Document Type

For the Convergent Invoicing, use the view V_TFK26EDOC in SM30. You can determine the invoice types (invoices, credit memo, or debit memo) for the process:

Create a New Class for eDocuments

To create a new class that inherited from SD/FI module, follow the following instructions:

  1. Open the class builder transaction (SE24). Name your class and click "Create".
    See the example for Mexico eDocument process.
  2. Create a CONSTRUCTOR parameter with the following parameters:

    Example of the constructor logic for country Mexico is as follows:

Redefine your Class based on the Business Requirements

  1. For redefining classes, you must implement the following two methods with different purposes:
    • IS_RELEVANT decides if the contract accounting (FI-CA) document is relevant for the eDocument creation
    • DETERMINE_EDOC_TYPE decides what kind of eDocument type process is triggered
  2. In your newly created eDocument class with prefix "Z", insert class CL_EDOC_PROCESS in the  'Friends' section as shown in the following example.

    After the above settings you are able to create eDocuments and view them in the  EDOC_COCKPIT transaction.

AIF mapping

  1. Open the Class Builder transaction (SE24) create a mapping class with a prefix "Z".
    For example ZCL_EDOC_MAP_MX_FICA), which is inherited from the FI mapping CL_EDOC_MAP_MX.
  2. Create static public method GET_SOURCE_DOCUMENT_DATA_FICA. This method is called from the interface connector to move data from eDocument to AIF mapping.
    See the example of data transfer logic in Mexico solution below:
  3. Redefine methods you need to adjust for contract accounting (FI-CA) fields.
  4. Go to the /N/AIF/CUST transaction.
    Select the Define Interface activity and search for the interface created for the SD/FI solution of the specific country. For example, /EDOMX is the SD/FI solution for Mexico.
    Select the mapping structure name EDOC_’XX’_MAPPING_SOURCE that enables the extension of contract accounting fields.
  5. Open the Business Add-in (BAdI) transaction (SE18) and enter EDOC_INTERFACE_CONNECTOR in the BAdI name fields to create a new implementation.
  6. Use the following filter criteria in the implementation.
    • COUNTRY = ‘<COUNTRY_CODE>’
    • GENERIC_FILTER = ‘ZCL_EDOCUMENT_<COUNTRY_CODE>_CA’
    • INTERFACE_TYPE = ‘STANDARD’

Note: This generic filter is also used for creating the CONSTRUCTOR parameter in the previous paragraph. (See the orange arrow in the following picture.)

Create a new Badi implementation class, example ZCL_EDOC_INTF_CONN_”XX”_FICA inheriting from CL_EDOC_INTF_CONN_”XX” from the FI/SD module.
Redefine the method GET_SOURCE_DATA to call the mapping class and move the data from eDocument (this refers to point 2). See the example of Mexico below:

Additional Customizing Activities

The following customizing activities might differ for various document creations. For Contract Accounting enter the source type FICA_DOC. For Convergent Invoicing enter the source type  CI_DOCUM.

  1. Determine the interface ID for the process step and source type with the view EDOPROCSPINTDETV in the transaction SM30.
    Sample values for Mexico:
  2. Use the view EDOPROCSTEPDETV to determine the process step for each Interface ID and source type.
  3. Use the view EDOINTAIFV to assign the AIF interface to the corresponding Interface ID.
  4. Use the view EDOSOASERV to enter the eDocument SOA Service Ports.
  5. Use the view EDOINTV to assign a SOA Service to each interface ID.
  6. Use the view EDOMAPCLASSDETV to assign a mapping class to each process, the Interface ID, and the source type.
  7. Activate the company code for specific source type using the view EDOCOMPANYACTIV for the eDocument creation.
    • For Contract Accounting, use FICA_DOC
    • For Convergent Invoicing, use CI_DOCUM

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments and let us know about your thoughts!

entry.title: Re: Why Data Readiness Is the Biggest Success Factor in SAP S/4HANA Migrations?
entry.content: Tks for this post.  A key point that is often underestimated is that data readiness is not only important for migration, but also for any kind of analytical or predictive use case after go-live. In procurement and supply chain scenarios, inconsistent master data or fragmented transactional history not only delay migration, but also limit the ability to identify patterns such as supplier performance or demand fluctuations. A common pitfall is treating data readiness as a one-time project activity. In practice, data quality needs to be continuously monitored and aligned with how data is actually created in daily operations. From an operational perspective, it is often more effective to start with a few critical data domains, such as vendor and material master data, and ensure they are consistent before expanding to broader datasets. This creates a stable foundation not only for S/4HANA processes, but also for any future analytics or AI-driven decision support.

entry.title: Questions About Display Options in the New SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management Form (New UI)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently working with the new SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management form (latest User Interface) and we have a few questions regarding UI display options. I would appreciate your help or confirmation on whether the following items are configurable:

1. Can the Goal/Target Category be displayed in view mode?

When editing a Performance Target, the “Category” dropdown is visible.
However, when simply viewing the target (read‑only mode), the category is not shown.
Is there a way to display the category in view mode in the new UI?

 

2. Can the icons on the goal sections be removed?

In the new form UI, section icons (such as the circular “info” symbol next to Position Goals or Corporate Targets) appear by default.
Is there any option to hide or remove these icons?

3. Can the stars and the strikethrough symbol in the Overall Form Rating be removed?

In the Summary section, the Overall Form Rating displays star icons as well as a grey strikethrough symbol.
Is it possible to hide or remove these rating symbols in the new Performance Management UI, or are they system‑controlled?

Many thanks in advance for your help.

Best Regards,
Johannes.

entry.title: Re: DG Exceptions in ECC
entry.content: It is no longer working as when we create Safety Data Sheets, the exceptions are correctly showing in the DG master and the SDS itself. However, once sent by SDS Distribution, the customer is getting something completely different. They then notify us and we send the correct document. As there is no historical knowledge in the business currently on how this was originally set up and customised, we have failed at troubleshooting and I have been advising to create one specification for the bulk material with the correct DG. And a separate specification for the packaged materials with the exemptions. Therefore the system cannot get confused where material assignments are mixed up with other data and assigned to multiple sources. Some of our products fall under UN1866 PG III. However, when the material is < 25kg and is neither Skin Corrosive nor Acutely Toxic, we can apply the exemption under ADR/ADN/RID (Viscous Liquids 2.2.3.1.5).

Kind regards,

Jana

entry.title: Re: Badi MD_EXT_SUP is not triggered in MD01n - MRP Live
entry.content:
Thanks for your blog.

We had a similar requirement as well, where we had to pick the issuing storage location from customizing and update in PR (EBAN-RESLO) in the BADI method : PPH_MRP_RUN_BADI->PURREQ_BEFORE_UPDATE_ADJUST.

In our case, the table field T460A-ADDIN contains the storage location, that we wanted to store in the PR -> Issuing storage location.

For example : In the case MRP run for Plant M004, with key M5, the BADI should set 1000 (from T460A-ADDIN last 4 characters)

Below is the SQL Script code that made it work for us. ✅

  METHOD if_pph_mrp_run_badi~purreq_before_update_adjust
         BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
         OPTIONS READ-ONLY USING t460a.

*{CHG0069051 - MRP Live - Badi to fill Issuing Storage location

-------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 1) Join IT_CTRL WITH T460A to fetch ADDIN and split it to get storage location
-------------------------------------------------------------------
    lt_reslo = SELECT DISTINCT
                        c.werks as werks,
                        c.sobsl as sobsl,
                        substring(a.addin, 5, 4)  AS reslo
                    FROM :it_ctrl AS c
                    INNER JOIN t460a AS a
                       ON a.werks = c.werks
                      and a.sobsl = c.sobsl;

--------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 2. UPDATE only reslo in lt_eban_new
--------------------------------------------------------------------
    lt_eban_new = SELECT the_index, doc_key_tmp, bnfpo, bsart, bstyp, bsakz, loekz, statu, estkz, frgkz, frgzu, frgst, ekgrp, ernam,
                        erdat, afnam, lastchangedatetime, creationdate, creationtime, txz01, matnr, ematn,
                        eban.werks,   --Plant
                        lgort, bednr, matkl, reswk, menge, meins, bumng, badat, lpein, lfdat, frgdt, webaz, preis, peinh, pstyp, knttp,
                        kzvbr, kflag, vrtkz, twrkz, wepos, weunb, repos, lifnr, flief, ekorg, vrtyp, konnr, ktpnr, infnr, zugba, qunum,
                        qupos, dispo, sernr, bvdat, batol, bvdrk, ebeln, ebelp, bedat, bsmng, lblni, bwtar, xoblr, ebakz, rsnum, sobkz,
                        arsnr, arsps, fixkz, bmein, revlv, vorab, packno,kanba, bpueb, cuobj, frggr, frgrl, aktnr, charg, umsok, verid,
                        fipos, fistl, geber, kzkfg, satnr, mng02, dat01, attyp, adrnr, adrn2, kunnr, emlif, lblkz, kzbws, waers, idnlf,
                        gsfrg, mprof, kzfme, spras, techs, mfrpn, mfrnr, emnfr, fordn, fordp, plifz, berid, uzeit, fkber, grant_nbr, memory,
                        banpr, rlwrt, blckd, revno, blckt, beswk, eprofile, eprefdoc, eprefitm, gmmng, wrtkz,
                        --Issuing Storage Location
                        CASE
                           WHEN f.werks IS NOT NULL THEN f.reslo
                            ELSE eban.reslo
                        END AS reslo,
                        --Issuing Storage Location
                            kblnr, kblpos, prio_urg, prio_req, memorytype, anzsn, mhdrz, iprkz, nodisp, srm_contract_id, srm_contract_itm,
                            budget_pd, eldat, iuid_relevant, sgt_scat, sgt_rcat, fsh_season_year, fsh_season, fsh_collection, fsh_theme
                       from :ct_eban_new as eban
                       left outer join :lt_reslo as f
                         on f.werks = eban.werks;

--------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 3. RETURN updated table
--------------------------------------------------------------------
    ct_eban_new = SELECT eban.* from :lt_eban_new as eban;

  endmethod.

In your case, you may want to derive the storage location from other source, so you can change the step-1 of the above code as per your customizing and use it in step-2.

To be honest, this was lot more simpler and quicker to achieve in ABAP and I really had to scratch my head whole day to make it work. In step 2, I had to list all the fields of CT_EBAN_NEW in the SELECT statement, even if I had to change only 1 field.

It is really annoying to deal with such a SQL script for just 1 field update, but there is no other choice, as the AMDP BADI is executed in HANA DB level, so ABAP code can't be used here whatsoever. 

Hope this will help.

Regards,

Bharat Bajaj

entry.title: SAP AI Core: Authentication Errors and How to Fix Them (Part 1 of 2)
entry.content:
SAP COMMUNITY  |  TROUBLESHOOTING  |  SAP AI CORE  |  PART 1 OF 2

SAP AI Core: Authentication Errors and How to Fix Them

Every error between your first curl command and a working bearer token — what causes them, what they actually mean, and the exact fix for each one. Part 1 of 2.

By Deepa Dorairaj   |  SAP AI Solution Architect  |  SAP AI Core Troubleshooting Series  |  Published 2026

TL;DR: Getting your first SAP AI Core bearer token working on Windows is harder than it should be. This article documents every authentication error between zero and a working token — PowerShell curl alias conflicts, quote character issues, port errors, missing OAuth path, empty responses, and RBAC denials — with the exact cause and fix for each. Part 2 covers deployment and configuration errors once authentication is working.

Why This Article Exists

The SAP AI Core documentation covers the happy path well. What it doesn't cover is the sequence of errors most practitioners hit on Windows before they ever get a working API call. These errors are not obscure — they come up consistently across different BTP setups, different teams, and different experience levels.

This article documents six authentication errors in the order they typically appear, based on real troubleshooting sessions. Each error has a clear cause and a specific fix. No generic advice — only what actually works.

Part 2 (Friday) covers what happens after authentication works: 404 on deployment calls, two-instance confusion, resource group provisioning failures, and orchestration vs foundation-models mismatches.

BEFORE YOU START 

What You Need

This guide assumes you have an SAP AI Core service instance on BTP with at least one service key created. From the service key JSON you need four values:

  • url — the OAuth authentication base URL (e.g. https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com)
  • clientid — your OAuth client ID
  • clientsecret — your OAuth client secret
  • serviceurls.AI_API_URL — your AI Core API base URL

The url field in your service key does NOT include /oauth/token. You must append it manually. This is the single most common cause of authentication failures and is covered in Error 5 below.

ERROR 1 OF 6  
  ERROR 1    PowerShell curl alias — -X not recognized    ParameterBindingException 
What You See
Invoke-WebRequest : A parameter cannot be found that matches parameter name 'X'.
At line:1 char:6
+ curl -X POST 'https://...authentication.../oauth/token'
What Causes It

PowerShell has a built-in alias called curl that actually runs Invoke-WebRequest — Microsoft's own HTTP cmdlet. Invoke-WebRequest uses completely different syntax and does not understand the -X flag that real curl uses. When you type curl in PowerShell you are not running curl.exe — you are running Invoke-WebRequest.

The Fix

Two options — use Command Prompt instead of PowerShell, or explicitly call curl.exe to bypass the alias:

# Option 1 — Open Command Prompt (cmd) instead of PowerShell
# All curl commands in this guide use double quotes — required in cmd
 
# Option 2 — Stay in PowerShell but force the real curl binary
curl.exe -X POST "https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_ID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

All remaining examples in this guide use Command Prompt syntax with double quotes. If you prefer PowerShell, add .exe after curl and the commands will work identically.

ERROR 2 OF 6  
  ERROR 2    Curly quotes / bad characters    curl: (3) URL rejected: Port number was not a decimal number between 0 and 65535 
What You See
curl: (3) URL rejected: Port number was not a decimal number between 0 and 65535
What Causes It

When you copy a curl command from a web page, Teams message, email, or PDF, the quote characters are often converted to typographic curly quotes (“ ”) instead of straight ASCII quotes (" "). Curly quotes look identical on screen but are invisible corruption to curl — it cannot parse the URL correctly and reports a nonsensical port error.

This error is almost always a quote character issue. The URL itself is correct.

The Fix

Do not paste the URL from a rich text source. Type the URL manually, or paste into a plain text editor (Notepad, VS Code) first to strip formatting, then copy from there into the terminal.

Also ensure all quote marks in your command are straight ASCII double quotes (") — not curly (“”) or single (‘’). In Command Prompt, single quotes are not valid string delimiters at all.

# Wrong — curly quotes from copy-paste (looks fine, breaks silently)
curl -X POST “https://...authentication.../oauth/token”
 
# Correct — straight ASCII double quotes
curl -X POST "https://...authentication.../oauth/token"
ERROR 3 OF 6  
  ERROR 3    Single quotes in Command Prompt    curl: (3) URL rejected 
What You See
curl: (3) URL rejected: Port number was not a decimal number between 0 and 65535
What Causes It

Single quotes work as string delimiters in PowerShell and Linux/Mac terminals. They do not work in Windows Command Prompt. If you copy a curl command that uses single quotes (common in documentation written for Linux) and run it in cmd, Command Prompt interprets the single quote as part of the URL string — causing the same port parsing error as curly quotes.

The Fix

Replace all single quotes with double quotes when running curl in Command Prompt:

# Wrong — single quotes (Linux/Mac syntax, fails in Windows cmd)
curl -X POST 'https://...authentication.../oauth/token' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
 
# Correct — double quotes (Windows cmd syntax)
curl -X POST "https://...authentication.../oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"

In Command Prompt, line continuation uses ^ (caret) not \ (backslash). If you copy Linux curl commands with \ at the end of each line, remove them and replace with ^ or put the entire command on one line.

ERROR 4 OF 6  
  ERROR 4    Empty response — no output at all    Command exits with no output 
What You See

The curl command runs, the prompt returns, but nothing is printed. No error, no response, no JSON — just silence.

What Causes It

Three common causes in order of likelihood:

  • The -d parameter contains an & character that Command Prompt interprets as a command separator — splitting your command into two separate commands, one of which runs silently
  • A missing closing quote somewhere in the command causes cmd to wait for more input silently
  • The response is very large and the terminal is suppressing it — redirect to a file to capture it
The Fix

The & in client_credentials&client_id=... must stay inside double quotes. If it's outside quotes, Command Prompt treats it as a command separator:

# Wrong — & outside quotes splits the command
curl -X POST "https://.../oauth/token" -d grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=abc
 
# Correct — entire -d value inside double quotes
curl -X POST "https://.../oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_ID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

If you suspect a large response is being suppressed, redirect output to a file:

curl -X POST "https://.../oauth/token" ... > output.txt

Open output.txt — the full response will be there regardless of terminal display limitations.

ERROR 5 OF 6  
  ERROR 5    401 Unauthorized — missing /oauth/token path    {"error":"unauthorized"} 
What You See
{"error":"unauthorized","error_description":"An Authentication object was not found in the SecurityContext"}
What Causes It

The url field in your AI Core service key JSON is the base authentication domain — it does not include the /oauth/token path. Many practitioners use this URL directly and hit 401 because they're calling the base domain rather than the token endpoint.

Compare these two:

# What's in your service key url field (base domain — not the token endpoint)
https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com
 
# What you need to call (token endpoint — append /oauth/token manually)
https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
The Fix

Always append /oauth/token to the url value from your service key. The SDK handles this automatically — but when calling directly with curl you must add it yourself:

curl -X POST "https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_CLIENTID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

A successful token call returns a JSON object containing access_token — a long base64-encoded string. This is your bearer token. Copy everything between the quotes after "access_token": and use it in all subsequent AI Core API calls. Tokens expire after 3600 seconds (1 hour) — generate a fresh one for each session.

ERROR 6 OF 6  
  ERROR 6    RBAC access denied on resource group creation    {"error":{"code":"403"}} 
What You See
{"error":{"code":"403","message":"RBAC: access denied"}}
What Causes It

RBAC access denied on a resource group PUT call means your service key does not have admin scope for this AI Core instance. Service keys are created with specific role assignments — a key created without aicore_admin scope can generate tokens and list resources but cannot create or modify resource groups.

This is not a token expiry issue and not a URL issue. The token is working — the credentials behind it simply don't have the permission required.

The Fix

First — check if a resource group already exists before trying to create one. Many AI Core instances provisioned by BTP have a default resource group already:

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

If a resource group already exists with status PROVISIONED — use it. There is no need to create a new one.

If you genuinely need to create a new resource group and are hitting RBAC denial, recreate the service key with admin permissions:

  1. BTP Cockpit → Services → Instances → your AI Core instance → Service Keys
  2. Delete the existing service key
  3. Create a new service key — confirm aicore_admin role is included in the parameters
  4. Generate a new token using the new service key credentials

Check existing resource groups before attempting to create new ones. Most RBAC errors on resource group creation are unnecessary — the default resource group already exists and has status PROVISIONED. The GET call above takes 10 seconds and saves significant debugging time.

QUICK REFERENCE 

Authentication Error Quick Reference

 

Error / Symptom

Root Cause

Fix

ParameterBindingException — -X not recognized

PowerShell curl alias intercepting command

Use cmd or call curl.exe explicitly

Port number not decimal between 0 and 65535

Curly quotes from copy-paste corrupting URL

Type URL manually or paste through plain text editor first

Same port error with single quotes

Single quotes invalid in Windows cmd

Replace all single quotes with double quotes

No output — command exits silently

& in -d value treated as command separator by cmd

Keep entire -d value inside double quotes

401 — Authentication object not found

Calling base auth URL without /oauth/token suffix

Append /oauth/token to url value from service key

403 — RBAC access denied

Service key lacks aicore_admin scope

Check existing resource groups first — recreate key if creation is genuinely needed

THE WORKING COMMAND 

Complete Working Token Call for Windows Command Prompt

Once all six errors are resolved, this is the complete working token call. All values in capitals are replaced with your service key values:

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_CLIENTID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

A successful response looks like this — the access_token value is your bearer token:

{"access_token":"eyJhbGci...very long string...","token_type":"bearer","expires_in":3599}

Copy the full access_token value. In Part 2 we use it to call model deployments, diagnose 404 errors, resolve two-instance confusion, and fix resource group provisioning failures.

If your response contains access_token — authentication is complete. Every error in this article is behind you. Part 2 covers everything from this point to a working model response.
